issue_title: All requirements are commented out after `pipenv lock -r`
issue_body:
Since the update to 10.X all our `requirements.txt` files get commented out because of this specific piece of code: https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/blob/master/pipenv/core.py#L802

It wants the dependencies to include `--hash` but generating the lock file does not populate the hashes. And in our situation we can't use hashes because our team is a mix of different linux distros and macOS, so hashes are useless.

I suggest adding a flag to the `lock` command to ignore the `'--hash' not in dep[0]` requirement.

Something like:

```python
if '--hash' not in dep[0] and not ignore_pip_hash_mode:
```

##### Describe your environment

1. OS version: macOS 10.13, Ubuntu 16.04, Arch Linux x86_64 Linux 4.15.5-1-ARCH
1. Python version: 3.6.3, 3.6.4
1. Pipenv version: v10.1.0

##### Expected result

Normal requirement file, with no requirement being commented out.

```
alembic==0.9.7
arrow==0.12.1
mako==1.0.7
markupsafe==1.0
munch==2.2.0
pymysql==0.7.11
python-dateutil==2.6.1
python-editor==1.0.3
pyyaml==3.12
six==1.11.0
sqlalchemy==1.1.15
ujson==1.35
```

##### Actual result

Commented out requirements

```
# alembic==0.9.7
# arrow==0.12.1
# mako==1.0.7
# markupsafe==1.0
# munch==2.2.0
# pymysql==0.7.11
# python-dateutil==2.6.1
# python-editor==1.0.3
# pyyaml==3.12
# six==1.11.0
# sqlalchemy==1.1.15
# ujson==1.35
```

##### Steps to replicate

Pipfile:
```
[[source]]
url = "https://our.custom.devpi.com/simple"
verify_ssl = true

[requires]
python_version = "3.6"

[packages]
alembic = ">=0.9,<0.10"
arrow = ">=0.12.0"
Munch = "*"
PyYAML = ">=3.12,<4"
sqlalchemy = ">=1.1,<1.2"
ujson = "*"
PyMySQL = ">=0.7,<0.8"

[dev-packages]
pytest = ">=3.0,<3.1"
pytest-cov = ">=2.4,<2.5"
flake8 = "*"
yapf = "*"
```

Commands:
```bash
$ pipenv lock -r
```

This works fine on 9.0.3, but not on 10.1.0. 
We started seeing the issue on 9.1.0 where only `six` would get commented out.

We've noticed that the issue does not happen (hashes get populated in the lock file) when using `https://pypi.python.org/simple` as a source url, but we have closed source packages that we can't make public so we have to use our own devpi server. And AFAIK hashes don't work across different OSes, so we'd still have an issue, albeit a different one.

##### Issue description
When I run ``pipenv check`` I get the following message:

```
Checking PEP 508 requirements...
Passed!
Checking installed package safety...
36351: cryptography >=1.9.0 resolved (2.3 installed)!
python-cryptography versions >=1.9.0 and <2.3 did not enforce a minimum tag length for finalize_with_tag API. If a user did not validate the input length prior to passing it to finalize_with_tag an attacker could craft an invalid payload with a shortened tag (e.g. 1 byte) such that they would have a 1 in 256 chance of passing the MAC check. GCM tag forgeries can cause key leakage.
```

According to the description the bug was fixed 2.3, which I have installed but it appears pipenv is flagging everything >=1.9.0.

Executing ``pipenv run safety check`` shows no vulnerabilities.

##### Expected result

Pipenv check doesn't report a false positive.

##### Actual result

Pipenv checks reports a false positive.

On https://docs.pipenv.org/ I read

> The use of Python 3 is highly preferred over Python 2, when installing Pipenv. Compatibility with **three** virtualenvs is greatly improved when using Python 3 as the installation target.

I don't understand the word "three" here. Is this a typo?

I am having a special setup. I have a docker in which I create an emulated ARM rootfs using QEMU. Inside the emulated rootfs , I am going to create a virtual environment using pipenv which I can use later on my ARM target.
What is happening now is that I am always receiving the following exception:

``` console
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/pipenv", line 11, in <module>
    sys.exit(cli())
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 722, in __call__
    return self.main(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 697, in main
    rv = self.invoke(ctx)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 1066, in invoke
    return _process_result(sub_ctx.command.invoke(sub_ctx))
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 895, in invoke
    return ctx.invoke(self.callback, **ctx.params)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/vendor/click/core.py", line 535, in invoke
    return callback(*args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/cli.py", line 416, in install
    selective_upgrade=selective_upgrade,
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/core.py", line 1789, in do_install
    skip_requirements=skip_requirements,
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/pipenv/core.py", line 641, in ensure_project
    crayons.green(shorten_path(path_to_python)),
TypeError: __str__ returned non-string (type NoneType)
```

As I traced the exception, I found out that problem here is that the python_version() method returns None in my case. Then as I use `docker exec` to run `pipenv install`, I think I am mixing different shell environment, therefore crayons.green cannot convert None type to string. My proposal would be simply just use `str` in all crayons e.g. `crayons.green(str(python_version(path_to_python)))`

issue_title: Traceback on dependency resolution error with editable git
issue_body:
When I have an editable git repository and another requirement that causes a dependency resolution failure in my Pipfile, I get a `FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory: '"/usr/bin/python3"': '"/usr/bin/python3"'` error.

My reproducer uses https://github.com/OddBloke/sphinx-git and python-novaclient==2.26.0 (which always causes a dependency resolution error, as mentioned in #1232).

(As far as I can tell, the git repository and the other requirement don't need to be at all related to end up with this traceback.)

##### Describe your environment

1. OS Type: Ubuntu 17.10
1. Python version: Python 3.6.3
1. Pipenv version: pipenv, version 9.0.1

##### Expected result

Dependency resolution error output, with no traceback.

##### Actual result

A traceback:

issue_title: bdist_wheel build uses too many cores when building wheel and crashes
issue_body:
I'm running inside a container with lots of CPU and not much RAM. In this case, I have 40 cores and 2GB of RAM. I realise this is quite asymmetric, but I don't control the infrastructure, and that is what I have been given. When I try to run an installer that builds a wheel (in my case, the problem was with uwsgi), it spawns 40 compiler processes, runs out of memory and then crashes. There doesn't appear to be a flag to limit the number of CPU jobs. Is this something I am missing?

Thanks,
Jeremy

Also, from here: https://github.com/pypa/pip/issues/5321

issue_title: find_packages_ns not present in 40.0.0, but added to documentation
issue_body:
Hi,

I see that the method **find_pacakges_ns** has been added to the documentation for the latest version.

[find_packages_ns](https://setuptools.readthedocs.io/en/latest/setuptools.html#find-packages-ns)

But the code is not included in the 40.0.0 release, although it is part of the **master** branch.

`>>> import setuptools`
`>>>`
`>>> setuptools.__version__`
`'40.0.0'`
`>>>`
`>>>`
`>>> setuptools.find_packages_ns()`

[__init__.py - 40.0.0](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/v40.0.0/setuptools/__init__.py)

[__init__.py - master](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/master/setuptools/__init__.py)

[find_packages_ns - Line: 121](https://github.com/pypa/setuptools/blob/master/setuptools/__init__.py#L121)

Support for **find_packages_ns** is the latest commit on this branch 19 days ago.

And 40.0.0 was released 14 days ago.

Seems like this commit was missed to be included in the release.


**_-jatin_**

issue_title: Specify exactly which `.fields` to use to LZ.
issue_body:
(reason: to get rid of the need for https://github.com/statgen/locuszoom/pull/102)

1. Keep a dictionary: phenotype -> fields needed

2. Keep an ordered dictionary: field -> template line

3. When somebody requests a LZ: send along the exact `.fields` and `tooltip_template` as JSON in the HTML.

4. When somebody requests a PheWAS: send along the exact `.fields` (union of all traits) and `tooltip_template` as JSON in the HTML.

issue_title: Support "expanded" Open Graph metadata based on og:type
issue_body:
Facebook Oepn Graph defines an [expanded version of embedded metadata](http://ogp.me/#types) depending on the value of `og:type`.

For example:

```
article - Namespace URI: http://ogp.me/ns/article#

    article:published_time - datetime - When the article was first published.
    article:modified_time - datetime - When the article was last changed.
    article:expiration_time - datetime - When the article is out of date after.
    article:author - profile array - Writers of the article.
    article:section - string - A high-level section name. E.g. Technology
    article:tag - string array - Tag words associated with this article.
```

This is used for example on nytimes.com. Snippet:

```
<meta property="og:url" content="http://www.nytimes.com/2016/12/15/arts/music/from-steet-theater-to-wagner-on-the-opera-stage.html" />
<meta property="og:type" content="article" />
<meta property="og:title" content="From Street Theater to Wagner on the Opera Stage" />
<meta property="og:description" content="Àlex Ollé brings an avant-garde sensibility to “The Flying Dutchman,” which he set in Bangladesh instead of Norway. The production opens in Madrid on Saturday." />
<meta property="article:published" itemprop="datePublished" content="2016-12-15T05:55:55-05:00" />
<meta property="article:modified" itemprop="dateModified" content="2016-12-15T06:19:30-05:00" />
<meta property="article:section" itemprop="articleSection" content="Music" />
<meta property="article:section-taxonomy-id" itemprop="articleSection" content="C5BFA7D5-359C-427B-90E6-6B7245A6CDD8" />
<meta property="article:section_url" content="http://www.nytimes.com/section/arts" />
<meta property="article:top-level-section" content="arts" />
<meta property="fb:app_id" content="9869919170" />
```

Currently (as I write these lines version 0.3.0a1) extracts raw `article:...` properties 

```
...
  'article:author': [{'@value': 'http://www.nytimes.com/by/raphael-minder'}],
  'article:collection': [{'@value': 'https://static01.nyt.com/services/json/sectionfronts/arts/music/index.jsonp'}],
  'article:modified': [{'@value': '2016-12-15T06:19:30-05:00'}],
  'article:published': [{'@value': '2016-12-15T05:55:55-05:00'}],
  'article:section': [{'@value': 'Music'}],
  'article:section-taxonomy-id': [{'@value': 'C5BFA7D5-359C-427B-90E6-6B7245A6CDD8'}],
  'article:section_url': [{'@value': 'http://www.nytimes.com/section/arts'}],
  'article:tag': [{'@value': 'Opera'},
                  {'@value': 'Bangladesh'},
                  {'@value': 'Madrid (Spain)'},
                  {'@value': 'Teatro Real'},
                  {'@value': 'Wagner, Richard'}],
  'article:top-level-section': [{'@value': 'arts'}],
  'fb:app_id': [{'@value': '9869919170'}],
  'http://opengraphprotocol.org/schema/description': [{'@value': 'Ã\x80lex '
                                                                 'OllÃ© brings '
                                                                 'an '
                                                                 'avant-garde '
                                                                 'sensibility '
                                                                 'to '
                                                                 'â\x80\x9cThe '
                                                                 'Flying '
                                                                 'Dutchman,â\x80\x9d '
                                                                 'which he set '
                                                                 'in '
                                                                 'Bangladesh '
                                                                 'instead of '
                                                                 'Norway. The '
                                                                 'production '
                                                                 'opens in '
                                                                 'Madrid on '
                                                                 'Saturday.'}],
  'http://opengraphprotocol.org/schema/image': [{'@value': 'https://static01.nyt.com/images/2016/12/16/arts/16ALEXOLLE1-INYT/16ALEXOLLE1-INYT-facebookJumbo.jpg'}],
  'http://opengraphprotocol.org/schema/title': [{'@value': 'From Street '
                                                           'Theater to Wagner '
                                                           'on the Opera '
                                                           'Stage'}],
  'http://opengraphprotocol.org/schema/type': [{'@value': 'article'}],
  'http://opengraphprotocol.org/schema/url': [{'@value': 'http://www.nytimes.com/2016/12/15/arts/music/from-steet-theater-to-wagner-on-the-opera-stage.html'}],
...
```
while they could use the type-dependent OGP namespace

I am not sure how the code actually works, but it would be great to be able to specify additional formats. For example, I often use the formats:
 
* `2016-07-28_090707` 
* `2016-07-28_090707_n0700` (where I use `n` for `-` even though `-` is an acceptable character, the opposite `+` isn't always)
* `2016-07-28_090707_p0700`

I do not know if you plan to make it easy to extend the formats. Personally, I am first attempting to parse with my own attempts and using `dateparser` as a fallback.

issue_title: Quoting error cited as indentation error
issue_body:
Trying to parse the document:
```
blah: 
  - "He said "Hi!" to his friends"
```
gives the following error:
```
bad indentation of a sequence entry at line 2, column 15:
      - "He said "Hi!" to his friends"
                  ^
```
This is really a quoting issue (inner quotes need to be escaped).  Is it possible to have the Error that gets thrown reflect that, instead of calling it an indentation error?

As of the latest commit (2c87fe4b), `Selector.extract` does not replace entities, while `Selector.re` does:
```python
>>> import parsel
>>> sel = parsel.Selector(text=u'<script>{"foo":"bar"baz""}</script>')
>>> sel.css('script::text').extract_first()
u'{"foo":"bar"baz""}'
>>> sel.css('script::text').re_first('(.*)')
u'{"foo":"bar"baz""}'
```

The related code is at [line 72 of parsel/utils.py](https://github.com/scrapy/parsel/blob/2c87fe4b19fdaea99636ff2b23597325fcb555a5/parsel/utils.py#L72)

However, in some specific cases (e.g. the example above), we may find it useful if:
- [`Selector.re`](https://github.com/scrapy/parsel/blob/2c87fe4b19fdaea99636ff2b23597325fcb555a/parsel/selector.py#L241) does not replace entities.
- [`Selector.extract`](https://github.com/scrapy/parsel/blob/2c87fe4b19fdaea99636ff2b23597325fcb555a/parsel/selector.py#L251) does replace entities.

What do you think there're optional arguments to be added to both functions controlling behavior of `replace_entities`?

In simpleconsumer I was passing a list of all partitions to reset. Sometimes (often but not always) it would lock up.  Adding traces I found that the lock up was at the line that reads:

```
if partition.fetch_lock.acquire(True):

In the `reset_offsets()` function.

issue_title: Make SimpleConsumer commit_offsets handle cluster updates too
issue_body: This is a relatively minor issue if running a pure `SimpleConsumer`, because the fetch workers will almost always resolve a `SocketDisconnectedError` before the auto-commit worker gets there. With an `RdKafkaSimpleConsumer` however, we don't run those fetch workers, so the auto-commit worker will have to fend for itself.

In streamparse 3.2 it doesn't look like there is first class support for cycles as there was with the clojure dsl.

If you are to attempt to define a cycle in the naive way, you will reference a variable before it is defined because declaration of inputs happens at class instantiation time. _this works for all graphs that can be topologically sorted_:
```python
	word_spout = WordSpout.spec()
	count_bolt = WordCountBolt.spec(inputs={word_spout: Grouping.fields('word')}, par=1)

	a_bolt = A.spec(name="A", inputs=[count_bolt["count->A"], b_bolt["B->A"]]) # here this fails because b_bolt is not defined
	b_bolt = B.spec(name="B", inputs=[c_bolt["C->B"]])
	c_bolt = C.spec(name="C", inputs=[a_bolt["A->C"]])
```

The current solution therefore is to init each bolt and then extend inputs:
```python
def extend_inputs(bolt, inputs):
	additions = bolt._sanitize_inputs(inputs)
	bolt.inputs = {**additions, **bolt.inputs} # only python >3.5
	bolt.common = ComponentCommon(inputs=bolt.inputs, streams=bolt.outputs, parallelism_hint=bolt.par, json_conf=bolt.config)

class WordCount(Topology):
	word_spout = WordSpout.spec()
	count_bolt = WordCountBolt.spec(inputs={word_spout: Grouping.fields('word')}, par=1)
	a_bolt = A.spec(name="A", inputs=[count_bolt["count->A"]])
	b_bolt = B.spec(name="B")
	c_bolt = C.spec(name="C")

	extend_inputs(a_bolt, [b_bolt["B->A"]])
# and so on...
```

issue_title: ellipse region to_mask() fails due to adding scalar to units value
issue_body:
In my first tentative experiments with regions (version 0.3.dev502, with astropy 2.0.1, numpy 1.3.3, and macOS 10.12.6), I read in an ellipse region from a DS9 region file (which seemed to succeed) and then tried to generate a mask. This failed:

```
In [6]: regList = regions.read_ds9(regionFile)
In [7]: ellipse_reg = regList[0]
In [8]: print(ellipse_reg)
Region: EllipsePixelRegion
center: PixCoord(x=306.1, y=292.1)
width: 330.0
height: 161.7
angle: 178.4

In [9]: ellipse_reg.to_mask()
```

The relevant part of the stack trace seems to be:

```
/Library/Frameworks/Python.framework/Versions/3.6/lib/python3.6/site-packages/regions-0.3.dev502-py3.6-macosx-10.6-intel.egg/regions/shapes/ellipse.py in bounding_box(self)
    118 
    119         t1 = np.arctan(-self.height * tan_angle / self.width)
--> 120         t2 = t1 + np.pi * u.rad
    121 
    122         dx1 = 0.5 * self.width * cos_angle * np.cos(t1) - 0.5 * self.height * sin_angle * np.sin(t1)
```
the trace ends with
`UnitsError: Can only apply 'add' function to dimensionless quantities when other argument is not a quantity (unless the latter is all zero/infinity/nan)
`

The problem seems to be that a standard numpy scalar float value is returned by np.arctan, and this gets added to the Units value np.pi * u.rad, triggering the exception. There's an identical computation at line 129. (My off-the-cuff reaction is that it's probably not necessary to use units for these calculations.)

issue_title: pip install spawns a lot of build_ext processes
issue_body:
When trying to run astropy's tests with tox, I got into some weird issue with I can reproduce simply with:
```
$ python setup.py sdist
$ pip install dist/astropy-1.1.dev14145.tar.gz
```
The `pip install` command will then spawn continuously `build_ext` processes until my system dies /o\ (See screenshot below). I guess this can be related to astropy's build system which detect than I run pip install with a development version, but the issue also happens if I run the pip install in a fresh virtualenv with only Numpy installed (no Cython or Jinja2).

![2015-11-25-152956_4800x1800_scrot](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/311639/11419547/748008a0-9428-11e5-8f8c-cf7367f5521a.png)

As astroquery has switched to continuous deployment, currently using the dev version numbering (x.y.devz) it became apparent that `z` in the version is nonsequential for master merges.

I see how two commits on two separate branches can have the same version number if the base of those branches is the same, but we saw this issue for the merge commits, too (see the dates of the releases here: https://pypi.org/project/astroquery/#history). I haven't investigated into the details, I can imagine it may still be an issue of travisCI (that we use for the deployments), but nevertheless the problem deserves an issue here.

cc @keflavich 

ps: We plan to switch away from using the dev version to do something more sequential for deploying, but that is yet to be implemented.

...and happy coding all the next `year++`!

### DRAFT

Just like we build and deploy NuPIC binaries for OS X and Linux, we should do the same for Windows. 

* * *

- [ ] `nupic.core` build creates binaries for win64 and uploads to S3
- [ ] NuPIC Appveyor build downloads and uses win64 `nupic.core` binaries
- [ ] Decide binary format for Windows releases (NuGet or pip)
- [ ] Create process to generate Windows binaries of NuPIC
- [ ] Update Appveyor CI job to create binaries on release tags

### Introduction to the GeospatialCoordinateEncoder

[See a video of @chetan51 describing the encoder here.](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KxxHo-FtKRo)

The [GeospatialCoordinateEncoder](https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/master/nupic/encoders/geospatial_coordinate.py#L36) (GCE) is a subclass of the [CoordinateEncoder](https://github.com/numenta/nupic/blob/master/nupic/encoders/coordinate.py#L29). Given a coordinate in an N-dimensional space, and a radius around that coordinate, the Coordinate Encoder returns an SDR representation of that position.

The Coordinate Encoder uses an N-dimensional integer coordinate space. For example, a valid coordinate in this space is (150, -49, 58), whereas an invalid coordinate would be (55.4, -5, 85.8475).

It uses the following algorithm:

1. Find all the coordinates around the input coordinate, within the specified radius.
2. For each coordinate, use a uniform hash function to deterministically map it to a real number between 0 and 1. This is the "order" of the coordinate.
3. Of these coordinates, pick the top W by order, where W is the number of active bits desired in the SDR.
4. For each of these W coordinates, use a uniform hash function to deterministically map it to one of the bits in the SDR. Make this bit active.
5. This results in a final SDR with exactly W bits active (barring chance hash collisions).

The GCE is given latitude, longitude, and speed. It calculates the radius for the CoordinateEncoder based upon the speed. Altitude is currently not a part of the GCE. 

### The Enhancement

This task is to add an optional altitude component to the GCE.

If the altitude is specified, the GCE should use a 3D coordinate when interfacing with the CoordinateEncoder. If the altitude is not specified, it should use a 2D coordinate. Ensure that the radius calculation based on speed of the CoordinateEncoder works in the 3D case (with altitude specified), and that it continues to adhere to the intention of `radiusForSpeed`, specifically in the amount of overlap it tries to achieve (as documented in the function currently).

### Tasks to be completed

- [ ] Capture NuPIC output from current GCE example code from sample route given in the [Geospatial Tracking App](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.geospatial). You'll want to save the anomaly scores for each point to compare results after you've made changes. 
- [ ] Before changing any production code, create unit tests that test input/output to the GCE, based upon the values you retrieved from the sample app above. Ensure they pass without changing the GCE.
- [ ] Update the GCE input tuple. Move speed to position 0, and add optional altitude parameter to the end. If tuple does not contain 4th parameter for altitude, it should use a 2D coordinate instead of 3D. Update all consumers of the GCE to adhere to the new format. Update documentation.
- [ ] If necessary, update the radius calculation to work for the 3D case as well.
- [ ] Ensure unit tests continue to pass.
- [ ] Create new unit tests to test that radius properly increases and decreases with changes in speed, with altitude specified. 
- [ ] Ensure new unit tests pass.
- [ ] Test end-to-end with the [Geospatial Tracking App](https://github.com/numenta/nupic.geospatial) codebase as-is.

### GPS Data

Sample data from @c4oe can be downloaded from [Google Drive](https://drive.google.com/file/d/0B38sjwW-2zVFRWhvbFlCWnRKM2c/view?usp=sharing).

The field labels are:

1. Message type: may be interrogation, squitter, or actual ADSB position message.
2. Status
3. Flight
4. Callsign
5. Mode-S Hex ID
6. Registration
7. Message date commence
8. Message time commence
9. Message date end
10. Message time end
11. Callsign 
12. Altitude
13. Speed
14. Heading
15. Latitude
16. Longitude
17. Vertical Speed
18. F-Flag
19. T-Flag
20. Vertical Speed Source
21. Mode 3

Most of these can be ignored. The important ones are `12. Altitude`, `13. Speed`, `15. Latitude`, & `16. Longitude`. Please note that you will need to pre-process this data to extract individual tracks for different aircraft. 

### Bounty

A [$500 bounty](https://www.bountysource.com/issues/5519417-geospatialcoordinateencoder-should-allow-optional-altitude) has been posted by @c4oe. See [more details on our mailing list](http://lists.numenta.org/pipermail/nupic_lists.numenta.org/2014-October/009803.html).

issue_title: encoder: process_batch_job gives wrong arguments to compose_file
issue_body:
In `process_batch_job`, `compose file` is called with directory, docname and extension as unnamed arguments [1], while the definition [2] takes only `dirname` and `extension` as unnamed arguments. This leads to `compose_file` returning `<directory>/<docname>.<docname>;<extension>` as path, instead of `<directory>/<docname>.<extension>`

[1] https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/blob/master/invenio/modules/encoder/batch_engine.py#L542
[2] https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio/blob/master/invenio/legacy/bibdocfile/api.py#L520

issue_title: alembic: wrongly naming the foreign key constraint fk_transaction_accounts_user_id
issue_labels: [{'id': 414883496, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-accounts/labels/Size:%20easy', 'name': 'Size: easy', 'color': 'bc3cbc', 'default': False}, {'id': 414883430, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-accounts/labels/Status:%20ready%20for%20work', 'name': 'Status: ready for work', 'color': '149414', 'default': False}, {'id': 209124211, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/inveniosoftware/invenio-accounts/labels/Type:%20bug', 'name': 'Type: bug', 'color': 'ff2a00', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
**Problem:**
The alembic recipe `9848d0149abd_create_accounts_tables.py` [creates a foreign key named `fk_transaction_accounts_user_id`](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-accounts/blob/a444115e0099cb311112273889edb3c9908ddb3c/invenio_accounts/alembic/9848d0149abd_create_accounts_tables.py#L98)  between the SQLAlchemy-Continuum's `transaction` table and the invenio-accounts'  `accounts_user` table.

The problem is that
- SQLAlchemy-Continuum [does not name the corresponding foreign key constraint in its model](https://github.com/kvesteri/sqlalchemy-continuum/blob/5f682e6e5bf038d8069db51e58f71d2c1dbed6c6/sqlalchemy_continuum/transaction.py#L150).
- invenio-db has a recipe `35c1075e6360_force_naming_convention.py` enforcing a naming convention and [renaming forcefully any constraint](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-db/blob/4df7ce16c2ae3f86f92596b1be4223f38604e543/invenio_db/alembic/35c1075e6360_force_naming_convention.py#L77-L88).
- the recipe `9848d0149abd_create_accounts_tables.py` [does not depend on recipe `35c1075e6360_force_naming_convention.py` but on a previous recipe](https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-accounts/blob/a444115e0099cb311112273889edb3c9908ddb3c/invenio_accounts/alembic/843bc79c426f_create_accounts_branch.py#L34).

Thus `9848d0149abd_create_accounts_tables.py` and `35c1075e6360_force_naming_convention.py` run in random order and force either the foreign key constraint name `fk_transaction_accounts_user_id` or `fk_transaction_user_id_accounts_user` (as generated by the naming convention). **Thus It is not possible to know which name the constraint will have after running `alembic upgrade`.**

**Solution.**
We should not name the constraint in `9848d0149abd_create_accounts_tables.py`. That way the resulting name will always be the one from the naming convention, i.e. `fk_transaction_user_id_accounts_user`, once the recipe `35c1075e6360_force_naming_convention.py` has ran. However it also means that as long as `35c1075e6360_force_naming_convention.py` is not called the name can vary. This should not be a problem as the module is still in beta and alembic upgrades are not yet officially supported.

cc: @lnielsen, @egabancho, @tiborsimko, @remileduc, @PXke, @kaplun

The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

issue_title: BTrees not finding contained keys on parallel access with gevent
issue_body:
This grows out of the discussion here https://github.com/zopefoundation/BTrees/issues/21 What happened in my program is, I have some greenlets writing changes to a `BTree`, and another greenlet committing the changes. Doing this with enough load, and the `BTree`s start to throw `KeyError`s when reading keys, they're supposed to contain. This only happens when using `ClientStorage` and a server, not on direct access to a `DemoStorage`. So please let me know if I should redirect this error report to the ZEO package.

I'm aware it's not an ideal setup, and I probably should have used a separate transaction manager for every single greenlet. Though, I was using this setup before on direct file access, and thought having the database run as a separate server process should be a drop-in replacement. Here the code:

```python
"""
As counterpart for this test, run a ZEO server with this config:

<zeo>
   address /tmp/zodbsock
</zeo>

<demostorage>
</demostorage>

"""

from gevent import monkey, joinall, spawn, coros, sleep
monkey.patch_all()

import logging
import random
import string

import transaction
from BTrees.OOBTree import OOBTree
from ZODB import DB, DemoStorage
from ZEO import ClientStorage


class Database(object):
    _closed = True

    def __init__(self, socket):
        self.socket = socket
        self.commit_lock = coros.RLock()
        self.open()

    def open(self):
        if not self._closed:
            return

        if self.socket:
            self.storage = ClientStorage.ClientStorage(self.socket)
        else:
            self.storage = DemoStorage.DemoStorage()

        self.db = DB(self.storage)
        self.tm = transaction.TransactionManager()
        self.connection = self.db.open(transaction_manager=self.tm)
        self.root = self.connection.root()
        self._closed = False

    def close(self):
        if self._closed:
            return
        self.tm.commit()
        self.connection.close()
        self.db.close()
        self.storage.close()
        del self.root
        del self.connection
        del self.db
        del self.storage
        self._closed = True

    def commit(self):
        with self.commit_lock:
            self.tm.commit()
            self.tm.begin()
            print("Commited")


NOBJS = 10000


def randstr(n):
    return ''.join(random.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))


def keyfunc(k1, k2='asdf', **kwargs):
    return str(k1) + '_' + k2


def init(sock=None):
    db = Database(sock)
    if 'test' in db.root:
        del db.root['test']
        db.commit()
    db.root['test'] = test = OOBTree()

    for i in xrange(NOBJS):
        test[keyfunc(i)] = randstr(20)
    db.commit()
    return db


def _stress_inner(tree, j):
    """ Read, updated and insert data """
    i = random.randint(0, NOBJS - 1)
    # Without reading, no errors happen
    _ = tree[keyfunc(i)]
    tree[keyfunc(i)] = randstr(30)
    # Adding no items reduces the KeyErrors
    tree[keyfunc(i + j * 1000, 'extra')] = randstr(20)


def stress(db, i):
    tree = db.root['test']
    for j in range(100):
        _stress_inner(tree, j)
        # Using a plain sleep() reduces the chance for KeyErrors
        sleep(random.random() / 1000)


def main():
    logging.basicConfig(level=logging.INFO)
    # Using plain init() and the KeyErrors are gone
    db = init('/tmp/zodbsock')
    try:
        stressers = [spawn(stress, db, i) for i in xrange(100)]
        while not all(s.ready() for s in stressers):
            db.commit()
            sleep(0.1)
        joinall(stressers)
        db.commit()
    finally:
        db.close()

if __name__ == '__main__':
    main()
```

issue_title: onResume deadlock with pyjnius/pygame/sdl on android
issue_body:
It seems that interaction between android.pyx, pyjnius and app pause mode can cause deadlock when going into pause mode or when resuming from pause mode.  I am using the pygame bootstrap.

A few logs and things below to show what's going on

Here's the typical sequence - first I press the home button and the app should pause.

```
I/python  ( 4288): [INFO   ] [Android     ] Must go into sleep mode, check the app
I/python  ( 4288): [INFO   ] [Android     ] App paused, now wait for resume.
I/System.out( 4288): python pyjnius java:invoke(<proxy>, public abstract void android.view.ViewTreeObserver$OnGlobalLayoutListener.onGlobalLayout(), [Ljava.lang.Object;@33d6fa5f)
```

Note that *after* the "app paused" message, the Java interface of pyjnius receives a call which it tries to push to Python.  The log message is from line 15 of NativeInvocationHandler.java of pyjnius

The above happens *before* I try to resume my app.

Once I try to resume, I get a black screen and nothing happens.  I have logging setup on the pygame bootstrap and I can see that the app's onResume() method in PythonActivity.java is never called.

I get the following log output

```
W/ActivityManager( 1005): Activity stop timeout for ActivityRecord{20c40984 u0 org.hottwaj.hottwajtest/org.renpy.android.PythonActivity t8207}
I/ActivityManager( 1005): Activity reported stop, but no longer stopping: ActivityRecord{20c40984 u0 org.hottwaj.hottwajtest/org.renpy.android.PythonActivity t8207}
W/ActivityManager( 1005): Activity pause timeout for ActivityRecord{20c40984 u0 org.hottwaj.hottwajtest/org.renpy.android.PythonActivity t8207}

I/WindowState( 1005): WIN DEATH: Window{2a469087 u0 org.hottwaj.hottwajtest/org.renpy.android.PythonActivity}
W/WindowManager( 1005): Force-removing child win Window{200ab265 u0 SurfaceView} from container Window{2a469087 u0 org.hottwaj.hottwajtest/org.renpy.android.PythonActivity}

```

Normally I can only trigger this outcome if I press the "home" button.  If I open the "apps task switcher" menu and then immediately switch back to my app, resume is handled correctly.

I believe this is a deadlock issue because occasionally an ANR crash occurs (not always though - probably because the call to NativeInvocationHandler.java does not always happen before app pause, but sometimes happens on resume).

Below is the ANR stack trace which sometimes occurs when the app is resumed and has been sat at black screen for a while - the crash occurs because pyjnius' NativeInvocationHandler.java can't get hold of a lock on a python thread (GIL maybe?)

It seems like either pyjnius or PythonActivity.java need to somehow "know" that the app is paused in order to somehow prevent this situation?

issue_body:
Not a python requests or pandas_datareader internals hacker here so perhaps I did something wrong. When I call web.DataReader without passing the session, it works fine, albeit without a session cache.

Thanks in advance to all those who make Pandas and the toolkits around it possible.

```

Name: pandas-datareader
Version: 0.5.0
```

```
#!/usr/bin/env python
import pandas_datareader.data as web
import datetime
import requests_cache
expire_after = datetime.timedelta(days=3)
session = requests_cache.CachedSession(cache_name='requests_cache', backend='sqlite', expire_after=expire_after)
start = datetime.datetime(1902, 1, 1) # earlier than 1902 causes the pandas datareader daily.py implementation to overflow an int
end = datetime.datetime.today()
df = web.DataReader('F', 'yahoo', start, end, session=session)
```
Yields

The installer for the message handler is called qInstallMsgHandler in PyQt4 and pyside and qInstallMessageHandler in PyQt5. In PyQt5 the message handler got an extra argument called context.

The reason to use qtpy is to have a program running in all three environments. Thus, there should be a unified way to use it (e.g. set context to None if pyqt4/pyside is used).

@andfoy, please release **0.1.2**. Important things to do:

1. Close this issue before creating our Changelog. An issue informing about a new release must be created every time so that the rest of the team knows about it.
2. Close the Github milestone before creating our Changelog so that it's created with the right date format.
3. Update our conda-forge feedstock.
4. Move conda-forge packages to the spyder-ide channel once they are uploaded to anaconda.org. Our installation instructions must not include conda-forge because installing from conda-forge is one of the main causes of breakage in Anaconda (when not done in a different environment from root, which almost nobody does).

I used the default settings for

gcab-0.7 (with option -z)
brotli-0.6.0
bzip2-1.0.6
gzip-1.8
zip-3.0
xz-utils-5.2.3
p7zip-16.02

190778	./goethe.txt
69034	./goethe.br

This is my test file: [goethe.txt](https://github.com/google/brotli/files/1373544/goethe.txt)

I used German as an example, because that language is spoken by about 100 Million speakers and it is the most most  widely  spoken  mother tongue in the European Union, even before English and French. (See [here](http://ec.europa.eu/commfrontoffice/publicopinion/archives/ebs/ebs_386_en.pdf#%5B%7B%22num%22%3A11%2C%22gen%22%3A0%7D%2C%7B%22name%22%3A%22XYZ%22%7D%2C68%2C771%2C0%5D)).

Thanks for the awesome tool. I am working on multilingual text data, specifically the mix of Chinese and English so english words would be used in between Chinese characters without any space delimiter like `有hockey羽毛球欖球籃球足球`. I don't have a lot of data like this. So I was wondering, will the tool work on such data if I fit in both chinese and english only text as input? If not, any insights on how this can be handled? Thanks a lot for your help in advance

issue_title: Create a directory for OCD-ID files and add DE files
geocoders.base.Geocoder._call_geocoder() does not sent HTTP headers breaking Nominatim usage
issue_body:
`osm.Nominatim` expects the `User-Agent` as HTTP header otherwise it returns a HTTP-403. But there is no way to get `_call_geocoder()` to send this header:
- `requester` is not set by `Nominatim.geocode`, so `urllib.urlopen` is used as requester. But if there is a requester only the `url` is sent but not the headers. See https://github.com/geopy/geopy/blob/master/geopy/geocoders/base.py#L122 and the following lines.
- I could hardly think of a use-case where `bool(requester)` is `False` but the requester is callable, I am not sure what the initial use-case was for the code. (#L143)

Locally I now use a `class` derived from `osm.Nominatim` which converts `url` to a `Request` (as in https://github.com/geopy/geopy/blob/master/geopy/geocoders/base.py#L137) before calling `_call_geocoder()` with this request object as url. As not knowing the use-case for the code in `_call_geocoder()` I do not feel empowered to write a pull-request to get this fixed in the library.

issue_title: Gérer l'hétérogénéité d'imposition des produits d'assurance-vie
issue_body:
Pour les années avant 2018, renseigner directement les variables `f2ee` et `f2dh` entraîne une valeur nulle de `prelevement_forfaitaire_liberatoire`. Cet impôt est calculé via les variables commençant par `assurance_vie`. Et le montant de ces variables est injecté dans `f2ee` ou `f2dh`. Justification : une même case peut contenir des revenus taxés à des taux différents (c'est le cas de la case `f2ee` mais pas de `f2dh`). Mais renseigner directement  `f2ee` ou `f2dh` sans renseigner les variables commençant par `assurance_vie` entraîne  `prelevement_forfaitaire_forfaitaire` = 0. Que faire par rapport à ça ? Coder de telle sorte que si AUCUNE variables commençant par `assurance_vie` n'est renseignée, un taux par défaut s'applique ?

La même logique est adopée pour le prélèvement forfaitaire unique.

issue_title: Ajout du régime des micro-bénéfices agricoles (depuis 2016 )
issue_body:
Comme décrit dans la PR #917, le **régime du forfait agricole** qui s'appliquait aux agriculteurs dont les recettes ne dépassaient pas un certain plafond est remplacé à partir de l'imposition des revenus 2016 par le **régime du micro-bénéfice agricole**.

La formule de calcul ainsi que les paramètres sont modifiés.

issue_title: Use pip dependencies in travis tests

This is an unwanted behaviour, as we know rely on continous releasing, and it could make the CI testing results erroneous.

issue_title: Sharding with range filters results in poor workload distribution
issue_body:
This is a somewhat old issue that I doesn't seem to have gotten much attention. I found a Google Groups post that articulates the problem and proposes a fix for it here: https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/app-engine-pipeline-api/O_5aaoNsS04

Problems summary:
Presently equality[sic] filters are ignored when choosing key range split points using the __scatter__ property. The resulting split points are appropriate for the entire population of entities of a given kind, but not necessarily for the sub-population matching the equality filter(s). This can result in a very uneven distribution of work among shards.

I thought I'd re-file it here in case that group is no longer monitored.
The fix seems simple enough and I'm happy to create a local branch with it, but the performance impact is huge enough that it would seem prudent to get it into the main branch.

I want to use `datastore` paging to fetch the query results. Specifically, I upload 11 entities to `datastore` and then want to fetch them (`limit=10`) using following code:
```python
query = <google.cloud.datastore.Client>.query(kind='test_kind')

query_iter = query.fetch(limit=limit, start_cursor=cursor)
page = next(query_iter.pages)
next_cursor = query_iter.next_page_token
entities = list(page)

logging.info('Cursor: %s, Next cursor: %s, Loaded entities: %d.',
              cursor, next_cursor, len(entities))
```
(where initially `cursor = None` and then is set to `next_cursor` value)

Unfortunately, I am getting not `None` value for the `next_cursor` even there are not any more results to fetch:
```
# First call, everything as expected
Cursor: None, Next cursor: foo, Loaded entities: 10.

# Second call, expected next_page_token = None
Cursor: foo, Next cursor: bar, Loaded entities: 1.

# Third call, again expected next_page_token = None
Cursor: bar, Next cursor: bar, Loaded entities: 0.
```

It seems that `next_page_token` is always not `None` and moreover, if there is not any result to fetch, `next_cursor == cursor`.

Setup:
```
Python 2.7.12
google-cloud-datastore==1.3.0
```

Is this expected? If yes, how can I recognise that there are not more results to fetch? 

I've checked the code briefly and it might be related to https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/blob/master/datastore/google/cloud/datastore/query.py#L457. It seems like `response_pb.batch.end_cursor == b''` does not hold and thus `next_page_token` is not set to `None`, but not sure why this is the case.

issue_title: Consider a heartbeat/monitor helper for subscriber
issue_labels: [{'id': 139685153, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20pubsub', 'name': 'api: pubsub', 'color': 'f8f8f8', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604243, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p2', 'name': 'priority: p2', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 810248285, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/triaged%20for%20GA', 'name': 'triaged for GA', 'color': 'b5a1ea', 'default': False}, {'id': 77438582, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/type:%20feature%20request', 'name': 'type: feature request', 'color': 'c5def5', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
It would extremely useful for users and for maintainers if pubsub had a sort of "heartbeat"/"monitor" helper. For example:

```python
subscription = subscriber.subscribe(...)
monitor = pubsub_v1.subscriber.Monitor(
    subscription,
    period=5 * 60  # Output once every five minutes.
)
monitor.start()

future = subscription.open()
future.result()
```

Every 5 minutes, the monitor would wake up and output the following to stderr:

```
# Pub/Sub Monitor for subscriber 0xDEADBEEF
subscription_name: /project/my-project/subscriptions/my-subscription
total_messages: 5000
ack_p99: 10s
outstanding_requests_p99: 10
...
```

Not sure how to best do this; but would be great with the ability to trigger dryRuns of jobs to get an idea of the amount of data it will consume and validate the query.

https://cloud.google.com/bigquery/docs/reference/v2/jobs#configuration.dryRun

... essentially it could be just a parameter on the job like for example write_disposition; however it might make more sense to do one of:

a) Add a bool param on the job "begin" method to allow dry-runs
b) Add a dry_run method that mimics begin but does a dry-run

I'm getting the following error when I try to build the docs.

```
Warning, treated as error:
/var/code/gcp/docs/trace/index.rst:22:failed to import google.cloud.gapic.trace.v1.trace_service_client
nox > Command bash ./test_utils/scripts/update_docs.sh failed with exit code 1
nox > Session docs failed. :(
Exited with code 1
```

This is also causing builds to fail for all PRs. I believe this was caused by https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/pull/4379

issue_title: Caching bug when creating python binaries when [python-repos] values are updated

issue_body:

Can support for this pattern be added into Pants with JUnit5 now being generally available? Thanks!

issue_title: v2 did you mean error message doesn't sort target names.
issue_body:
```
$ ./pants test  tests/python/pants_test/backend/jvm/tasks:has_a_typo

ResolveError: "has_a_typo" was not found in namespace "tests/python/pants_test/backend/jvm/tasks". Did you mean one of:
... 58 unsorted names ...
```

This can make finding the right one difficult

Although they're not perfect, allocation profiles from a few different phases during the lifecycle of `./pants list ::` indicate that the majority of both held and emphemeral allocation happens inside of `nodes::Context::get`, which is where `Graph` manipulation occurs.

Thought process:
  1) can we use raw references to a thing in order to avoid needing to own/clone it (without the lifetime management being a pain in the butt)?
  2) if it is impossible/impractical to hold a reference, is it large/copied enough to be worth moving into an `Arc`?
  3) if it needs to be owned, should it be in a Box in order to make the average size of an enum smaller?
  4) if it's ephemeral but occasionally large, should it move into a [thread local](https://doc.rust-lang.org/std/thread/struct.LocalKey.html)?

A summary of `std::mem::size_of` for a few relevant types:

    size	type
    24	DepSet
    24	Entries
    216	Entry
    16	EntryStateField
    24	Nodes
    16	NodeFuture<NodeResult>
    120	NodeKey
    24	ReadLink
    24	Stat
    24	Scandir
    72	Select
    112	SelectDependencies
    48	SelectLiteral
    88	SelectProjection
    48	Snapshot
    96	Task
    64	NodeResult

Note: this is their _shallow_ size: if they contain a `Box` (and thus also`Arc`/`Vec`/`String`/etc), those are not counted.

issue_title: Validate test directory structure before attempting to run python tests.
issue_body:
If tests are layed out in a nested directory structure, it's natural to assume typical python package structure - with `__init__.py`s - is appropriate. However, pytest does not like this. See https://pytest.readthedocs.io/en/reorganize-docs/new-docs/user/directory_structure.html.

Pants should probably validate that python test code, when residing in a source root separate from production code, has no packages to avoid confusing errors otherwise propagated by pytest.

issue_title: pantsbuild docker image not pullable due to permissions?
issue_body:
`build-support/docker/pants_dev/Dockerfile` states:

```
# To use on MacOS:
#   - Install Docker for Mac (https://docs.docker.com/docker-for-mac/).
#   - docker run -ti pants_dev
```

but in practice, this seems to fail with a permissions error when attempting to pull directly from the hub without a fresh local build:

```
[omerta pants (master)]$ docker run -ti pants_dev
Unable to find image 'pants_dev:latest' locally
docker: Error response from daemon: pull access denied for pants_dev, repository does not exist or may require 'docker login'.
See 'docker run --help'.
```

cc @stuhood @jsirois

As a thought exercise, it's time to design a protocol for "multi" machine support. But to keep things simple (and in particular, avoid thinking about membership/etc), let's assume only two machines: the box requesting the work, and the box doing the work.

The `multiprocessing` engine has been helpful to keep us cognizant about distribution, but it's time to think more deeply about this. In particular, in cases where a remote worker does not yet have the content for a `Subject`, the protocol to request execution of a `Step` might be a multi-round affair where the caller sends: "execute this if you have these input digests", the remote responds: "nope, don't have 0xAF", and the caller tries again with the extra content.

Let's not get into serialization, or choosing RPC frameworks, or anything like that. But let's design the protocol for this, and think about when we should be sending data, vs putting it in shared storage and having the other process consume it.

I get the following error if there is a '#pivot' in the URL for an explorer page such as on a playground:

````
VM453:1 Uncaught DOMException: Failed to execute 'atob' on 'Window': The string to be decoded is not correctly encoded.(…)
````

I tracked it to explorer.js line 225. Putting a breakpoint there I can see the following values:

````
> pivotState
"#pivot"
> pivotState.substr(1)
"pivot"
> atob(pivotState.substr(1))
VM453:1 Uncaught DOMException: Failed to execute 'atob' on 'Window': The string to be decoded is not correctly encoded.(…)
````

So Chrome doesn't like doing `atob` on the string "pivot". Removing the '#pivot' from the end of the URL fixes the problem.

Seems like either 'pivot' should be special cased to be ignored since it is used as a hash, or the pivot bookmark should be prefixed with 'pivot-' which can then be looked for to detect pivot bookmarks versus other hashes which may come up or be added in the future.

Regardless, the `atob` call should be wrapped in a try/catch to prevent it from breaking all JS on the page if a bad hash is encountered.

issue_title: Incorrect Version and Alias references with !Ref
issue_body:
Hi,
I just wanted to let you know that there seems to be a mistake in the example code to assume a serverless function.

In "Ref": "MyLambdaFunction.Version", Ref is not meant to return a specific attribute but a default value, depending on the resource that is referenced, such as ARN or ID. Ref should be replaced by Fn::GetAtt.

Source:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-lambda-version.html

In "Ref": "MyLambdaFunction.Alias", .Alias should be removed as the only return value is Alias ARN which is returned by a simple Ref.

Source:
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSCloudFormation/latest/UserGuide/aws-resource-lambda-alias.html

Thank you.

issue_title: Python Encryption Sample: KMS Key ID not found
issue_body:
Thanks for the great work in this project. I am trying to get the python encryption/decryption sample running. Seems like the code for encrypting a message cannot find the KMS encryption key. Invoking the function with a simple test message gives the following result: 
{
  "stackTrace": [
    [
      "/var/task/encryption.py",
      40,
      "post",
      "raise Exception(\"KMS Key ID not found.\")"
    ]
  ],
  "errorType": "Exception",
  "errorMessage": "KMS Key ID not found."
}
This is the code that throws the exception: 
def post(event, context):

    try:
        key_id = os.environ['keyId']
        message = event['body']
        if message is None:
            raise ValueError
    except KeyError:
        raise Exception("KMS Key ID not found.")
    except ValueError:
        return bad_request
Seems like the code expects an environment variable named keyId to be set, yet there is none. Any hints?

I've added
`Globals:
  Function:
    Handler: index.handler
    Runtime: nodejs6.10
    MemorySize: 128
    Timeout: 60`
to my template but was wondering why the function timed out after 3 seconds. Had to add a time-out to the resource itself.

issue_title: The FFMPEG reader fetches frames with the wrong time
issue_body:
If I want the 300th frame and I call `myreader.get_data(300)` I will not get the 300th frame, but the frame from one second before. This is because [this line in ffmpeg.py](https://github.com/imageio/imageio/blob/3db112824c336f9b3336149fcc44359e58642a88/imageio/plugins/ffmpeg.py#L292) reads

    iargs = ['-ss', "%.03f" % (starttime-offset)]

Where the offset is 1. Actually, after reaching starttime-offset, it should advance of 'offset' seconds to be exactly on 'starttime', like this:

    i_arg = ['-ss', "%.06f" % (starttime - offset),
          '-i', self.filename,
          '-ss', "%.06f" % offset]

See [here](https://trac.ffmpeg.org/wiki/Seeking#Combinedseeking) for details and explanations.
I have code in zoof that can read/write ICO, BMP, PNG in pure Python. Limited to RGB(A) though. But might be interesting to not need freeimage for png. And also for  #21

issue_title: poor user-facing handling of incomplete channel imports
issue_body:
For typical scenarios, a user sees the following chain of events on channel import:

* Manage
  * initiate channel import
  * see progress indicator
  * channel appears in channel list
* Learn
  * channel appears in switcher
  * content is available to be browsed

However, it is possible to deviate from this flow in a couple ways:

 * User refreshes or opens Manage in a new tab. They will see the new channel in the list, with no indication that it's only partially imported.
 * Another user browses Learn while import is in progress. They will see the new channel, but fail to load content with 404s

We need to better handle "partially imported" channels for both learners and admins.

issue_title: The points at the top shows up only when a kid engages with the content and attains mastery.
issue_body:
## Summary

When logging in as a learner in the points section on the top right corner is hidden and appears when a learner masters a content.

The point that appears is just for that content and if the learner navigates to profile section or another page the points disappear again. 

## System information
 - Version: Kolibri 0.4.0.beta1
 - Operating system: Ubuntu 14.04 LTS
 - Browser: Chrome


## How to reproduce
1. Login as a learner, observe that the points not the top right corner is not present.
2. Master a content and then observe that 500 points come up.
3. But if you navigate to profile section and come back to learn tab the points section disappears.

**Expected Result** 
Its better to show the total points and keep adding the incremental points as and when the kid masters a content, like it was in other Alpha release. 

**Actual Result**
This is something new in beta we observed and the points section is not consistent and appears only when a kid masters a topic.

issue_title: Create a Single Page App Kolibri Module Subclass
issue_body:
## Summary

The vast majority of frontend code in Kolibri is accessed via module that acts as the driving Single Page App for that particular URL endpoint.

These SPAs all define the following functionality:
- URL Routes under their Django defined entry points
- Instantiating a Vuex Store for client side state management
- An entry component that acts as an initial render point and controller for the SPA

As such, I propose we create a SinglePageApp subclass of the KolibriModule class that implements the following functionality:
- Router initialization based on a `routes` hash
- Store initialization based on the core store module and the SPA's own store (ideally, this would depend on #2022, and not actually require the initialization of the store to be delegated to the SPA, rather the SPA would register these new Vuex modules against a store that has already been instantiated in the core app - using the Vuex2 functionality that allows for dynamic registration of modules).
- Initializing and attaching the entry component

issue_title: Potential for further spatial optimization of generated CDXJ
issue_body:
There exists the potential for further de-duplication of CDXJ entries, particularly from multiple capture sources. Two WARCs, with entries containing headerT1/payloadX and headerT2/payloadX could potentially be merged, as the payload/response body is the same.

This might also have ramifications of squashing important information that resides in the headers that cause browsers to behave differently on replay based on which header is served.

This should be investigated further and provided as an option.

Credit to @b5 for suggestion.

issue_title: Provide full-text search of documents described in CDXJ
issue_body:
At one point, https://github.com/ipfs-search/ipfs-search allowed searching of the content of dereferenced payloads described by a set of IPFS hashes. Because IPFS hashes are describing non-changing content, being able to search the contents needs only a single iteration of indexing.

One initial target audience for ipwb is for smaller collections of archival content, identified through our conventional CDXJ indexing and replay procedure.

Provide a mechanism to allow a user to search the contents of the payloads identified in the currently loaded CDXJ. @ibnesayeed Please provide insights on this.

issue_title: Update to latest scikit-learn release for deprecation and compatibility
issue_body:
Using the current head 0.2.0 release of spark-sklearn and the current release of scikit-learn (0.18.1), I'm getting the following deprecation warning:

/.../python3.4/site-packages/sklearn/cross_validation.py:44: DeprecationWarning: This module was deprecated in version 0.18 in favor of the model_selection module into which all the refactored classes and functions are moved. Also note that the interface of the new CV iterators are different from that of this module. This module will be removed in 0.20.
  "This module will be removed in 0.20.", DeprecationWarning)

the library needs to be updated to use the new model_selection module and iterator interfaces.

In addition, due to changes in sklearn.model_selection.GridSearchCV, the attributes available on the fitted spark-sklearn.GridSearchCV are out of date.

sklearn.model_selection.GridSearchCV now has:

- cv_results_ : dict of numpy (masked) ndarrays - A dict with keys as column headers and values as columns, that can be imported into a pandas DataFrame.
- best_estimator_ : estimator - Estimator that was chosen by the search, i.e. estimator which gave highest score (or smallest loss if specified) on the left out data. Not available if refit=False.
- best_score_ : float - Score of best_estimator on the left out data.
- best_params_ : dict - Parameter setting that gave the best results on the hold out data.
- best_index_ : int - The index (of the cv_results_ arrays) which corresponds to the best candidate parameter setting.
- scorer_ : function - Scorer function used on the held out data to choose the best parameters for the model.
- n_splits_ : int - The number of cross-validation splits (folds/iterations).

While spark-sklearn.GridSearchCV has:

- grid_scores_ : list of named tuples
- best_estimator_ : estimator - Estimator that was chosen by the search, i.e. estimator which gave highest score (or smallest loss if specified) on the left out data. Not available if refit=False.
- best_score_ : float - Score of best_estimator on the left out data.
- best_params_ : dict -  Parameter setting that gave the best results on the hold out data.
- scorer_ : function - Scorer function used on the held out data to choose the best parameters for the model.

The most critical difference is that sklearn added the more comprehensive cv_results_ which adds data that the formerly compatible grid_scores_ is lacking.

issue_title: Unable to create Service in `global` mode
issue_body:
Attempting to create a service with `mode="global"` returns an error from the Docker API:

```
cannot unmarshal string into Go value of type swarm.ServiceMode
```

The build image function is missing some arguments that are present in the v1.24 api.

* memswap - Total memory (memory + swap), -1 to enable unlimited swap.
* cpushares - CPU shares (relative weight).
* cpusetcpus - CPUs in which to allow execution (e.g., 0-3, 0,1).
* cpuperiod - The length of a CPU period in microseconds.
* cpuquota - Microseconds of CPU time that the container can get in a CPU period.
* buildargs – JSON map of string pairs for build-time variables. Users pass these values at build-time. Docker uses the buildargs as the environment context for command(s) run via the Dockerfile’s RUN instruction or for variable expansion in other Dockerfile instructions. This is not meant for passing secret values. Read more about the buildargs instruction
* shmsize - Size of /dev/shm in bytes. The size must be greater than 0. If omitted the system uses 64MB.
* labels – JSON map of string pairs for labels to set on the image.

See: https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/api/docker_remote_api_v1.24/#/build-image-from-a-dockerfile

issue_title: Change shared memory in container when creating a container via docker Client
issue_body:
The current version of docker allows the user to change the size of shared memory inside a container, via ```
docker run --shm-size
```
but the docker-py `Client.create_container()` does not support this yet. 

This is bound to happen again as docker adds more features, so maybe you should consider taking *args into `Client.create_container()` which would be parsed directly into the `docker run` command.

issue_title: Feature Request: Add dtype to BinaryQuadraticModel construction
issue_body:
Right now the biases in `BinaryQuadraticModel`s can be arbitrary types which is convenient in many cases, however there are times that it would be nice to be able to specify a consistent type for biases.

**Proposed solution**
Add a `dtype` kwarg to the bqm construction methods.

```
bqm = BinaryQuadraticModel(linear, quadratic, offset, vartype, dtype=float)
```
The default value would be `dtype = object` which would be the current behaviour. In general we would assume that it is a callable (e.g. `float`, `int`, `np.int8`).

It would be nice if we also allowed [numpy data type](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/numpy/reference/arrays.dtypes.html) syntax so
```
bqm = BinaryQuadraticModel(linear, quadratic, offset, vartype, dtype='float64')
```
would be valid.

The implementation of wamp.auth.derive_key can raise a segfault if used with a high number of iterations (~ 100000).

issue_title: Rolling theta entropy with new pandas version
issue_body:
In the function `min_rolling_theta_entropy` from the motion module, this last line uses the old implementation of pandas rolling. 
https://github.com/soft-matter/trackpy/blob/964a3aec9ef4a1894713afb4dbb215bb57dbaedc/trackpy/motion.py#L589
It gives this error because I have pandas 0.23.1  
```python
AttributeError                            Traceback (most recent call last)
<ipython-input-3-bacdcaf009b9> in <module>()
----> 1 tp.motion.min_rolling_theta_entropy(proces.traj)

~/trackpy/trackpy/motion.py in min_rolling_theta_entropy(pos, window, bins)
    587     bins = np.linspace(-np.pi, np.pi, bins + 1)
    588     f = lambda x: shannon_entropy(x, bins)
--> 589     return pd.rolling_apply(direction.dropna(), window, f).min()
    590 
    591 
```
Trackpy has the new implementation of the pandas rolling. Shouldn't the last line be
```python
return pandas_rolling(direction.dropna(), window, f).min()
```

This is more of a long-term goal. It is possible to refine feature locations not by weighted averaging, but by least-squares fitting with a model of how all _n_ particles should appear. This was developed by Mark Shattuck's group for close granular packings; there is a tutorial (and Matlab code) [here](http://gibbs.engr.ccny.cuny.edu/technical/Tracking/ChiTrack.php). And you can also check out a [paper](http://link.aps.org/doi/10.1103/PhysRevE.75.051311) that uses the technique successfully.

I wanted to create an issue because I hope that someday a user will contribute this to trackpy. Maybe you!

issue_title: to_number does not correctly handle floating point numbers without dots
issue_body:
The [JMESPath specification of the to_number function](http://jmespath.readthedocs.io/en/latest/specification.html#to-number) states that everything matching the `json-number` production must be supported. However, this implementation will handle floating point numbers incorrectly if they do not contain a dot.

Please consider the following example
```json
{"x": "1e+21"}
```

When searched with the JMESPath
```
x | to_number(@)
```

this implementation will return None since the number is not recognized as such. The parser will only try parsing a floating point number if it contains a dot, as can be seen [in the code](https://github.com/jmespath/jmespath.py/blob/develop/jmespath/functions.py#L205)

Therefore, the following (incorrect) behavior can be observed
```python
$ python
Python 2.7.12 (default, Nov 19 2016, 06:48:10) 
[GCC 5.4.0 20160609] on linux2
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import jmespath
>>> data = {"x": "1e+21"}
>>> jmespath.search("x | to_number(@)", data)
>>> 
```

Please note the None return value which is not printed by the REPL.

The JSON standard allows such numbers, as per http://www.json.org/ where the grammar allows a special case of number `number := int exp` where `exp := e digits` and `e := 'e+'`.

For uniformity with the TransferClient accepting a UUID type for endpoint IDs, single-identity calls to `get_identities` should be allowed to pass a UUID.

We can also expand the set of types it takes. For example, iterables of strings or of UUIDs are things which we can handle if desired.
Mixed type iterables are kind of nasty, but it doesn't hurt us much to support them if we want (in fact, it might be harder to ensure that we _aren't_ getting them).

issue_body:
### What is wrong?

It seems that we currently do not have any unit tests for our opcodes. While we obviously have some higher order tests that indirectly cover a wide range of opcodes, I believe we should have some dedicated unit tests for our opcodes mainly to formally verify they work as intended.

### How can it be fixed

:rocket:

### What is the big idea!?

I have been thinking about a new API for *plugins* in the trinity client.

Plugins could range from things like simple internal optional components such as a gas estimation engine up to full blown applications like daemon processes for something like augur or ethereum alarm clock.

I also think that we might benefit from dog-fooding this API for many of our internal things such as the JSON-RPC server, the transaction pool, peer management for rating and blocking peers.

Here is a quick list of things that I think fit well into a *plugin* type architecture.

1. Application which manages paid slots for light peers
2. Transaction Pool
3. Rating peers
4. Blocking peers
5. Whisper
6. Deamon processes (augur, ethereum alarm clock, truebit)
7. The python console.

APIs that would need to be exposed for these things would be:

- hooks to add new sections of CLI arguments 
- hooks to add new CLI commands.
- high level Web3.py connection
- low level chain and database access
- hooks for interacting with core components like peer pool
- hooks for launching JSON-RPC server namespaces.

We might even be able to go as far as proving the ability to run new DevP2P services which would allow running something like whisper.

And finally, while this is the absolute extreme version of this idea, we may even be able to move syncing to be relegated to run within this system.


### How can it be fixed

This probably starts with a POC for either the transaction pool or the planned system for rating and persisting information about peers.

issue_title: Proposal: Vyper versions supported over HTTP API
issue_body:
### What was wrong?
The biggest problem with Vyper is that it's Python, and arbitrary/multiple version handling in Python is a hard thing to handle. It might just be better to delegate to dedicated servers that compile vyper programs using specific versions of the compiler. Access to these could be provided over an HTTP API (that's programmable), with an option to use a local version as well.

Also, with snapcraft/docker containerization, we may be able to support local versioning here too.
---

@fubuloubu
Hmm, maybe that is the solution to online integration: a JS "compiler" that just sends everything to Vyper instances on AWS.

issue_title: Auto-detect providers if none provided to Web3
issue_body:
* Version: 4

### What was wrong?

More and more functions are valuable even if not connected to a node. Initializing with no providers available would be helpful to access those functions.

Additionally, it would be natural to try to auto-detect providers if none are provided, equivalent to how `from web.auto import w3` works.

### How can it be fixed?
#### Todo

- [x] Review/improve error message when trying to connect to one of the providers and it is unavailable.
- [x] add default value to `providers` in `Web3` and `Manager`, to put the request manager in a special auto-detect mode
- [x] move autodetection logic from `web3.auto` into `web3.providers.auto`
- [x] auto-detect mode: save last-working provider to reattempt first
- [x] auto-detect mode: if no previously-working provider, or last-working is unavailable, re-attempt detection
~- [ ] auto-detect mode: if failure to detect, raise an exception describing it different from the first case~
- [x] auto-detect mode: look up environment variable first
- [x] replace `web3.auto.w3` implementation with `w3 = Web3()`
- [x] map environment URI to provider

#### Specifying No Providers

To prevent connection to any node, allow initialization of `Web3` with `None` provider specified. This is already technically supported, it just fails with unhelpful error messages at the moment.

```
from web3 import Web3
w3 = Web3(providers=None)
# or simply
w3 = Web3(None)
```

If any call attempt is made, it will always fail with an error saying that `Web3` explicitly disallows node interaction when started with `Web3(providers=None)`.

#### Auto-detecting Providers

To auto-detect providers, allow initialization of `Web3` with no provider specified:

```
from web3 import Web3
w3 = Web3()
```

A `Web3` instance is returned immediately, and can be used for anything that does not interact with the node.

The first time a call to a node is attempted, it tries to auto-detect a node. If one is found, save it for later use and automatically reuse it. In this mode, any time the connection fails, it will try to reconnect to any node that it can find.

#### Auto-detection in `web3.auto.w3`

```
from web3.auto import w3
```

Will now be exactly equivalent to:

```
from web3 import Web3
w3 = Web3()
```

That means two changes:

1. If no nodes are found, the import still succeeds and works for functions that don't need node interaction
2. If a node is not found at import time, `w3` will attempt to re-detect it on every attempt to call to the node

#### Explicitly Setting Providers

*No change in functionality*

For the normal use case (previously, the only explicitly supported use case), `Web3` would only attempt to use the providers specified. If none of them are available, it will **not** attempt to auto-detect another one. (If you try to connect to a testnet node and fail, we don't want to automatically connect you to a production node without you realizing)

issue_title: _'s in versions are not accepted by conda 4.3
issue_body:
I think this needs to be considered a blocker for 4.3 as it means that environments which were previously considered OK are now not OK.

When `conda skeleton cran` fetches r-packages from CRAN it replaces any `-` in the version with `_` (and helpfully puts a comment to that effect in the recipe), e.g.:

```
package:
  name: r-bit
  # Note that conda versions cannot contain -, so any -'s in the version have
  # been replaced with _'s.
  version: "1.1_12"
```

and also in the dependencies we also see such versions:

```
requirements:
  build:
    - r-base
    - r-bit >=1.1_12
```

Now, when conda is asked to install such dependencies I am getting:
```
failed to parse packages from exception: Value error: invalid package specification: r-bit=>=1.1_12
```
